title: artemisia vulgaris subsp. heterophylla
intro: Artemisia vulgaris subsp. heterophylla, also known as southern wormwood, is a subspecies of the common wormwood plant native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of southern wormwood include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as its potential to act as a natural insect repellent. Southern wormwood can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk remedies to treat various health issues.
